[PDF_PAGE 145 | score=12] EQUIPMENT changed centuries Rules for the treatment of injuries can be found in Healing; p. 123_ either draw powe by plugging into cockpit powcr jack use its internal battery; which works for Up to 48 hours_ The pack can dispense up to dozen doses of medications before needing replenishment, though these medications must replaced at least once month whether used Or not,. medipack will deliver dose of medication each time its wearer takes wound Or gets stunned. The dose will prevent the character from being stunned ad will allow him to ignore all TN modifiers caused by Minor Wounds or less (Serious Wounds worse have their usual effects) The wound value of all wounds still counts as usual. Finally: any Knockout Checks are made with Modifier to the TN, So long as the doses have not all been used UP, BASIC EQUIPMENT The most basic of medical equipment enough treat common injuries the case of serious and life-threatening injuries; at best these kits can be stopgap measure that can Keep person alive Iong enough for someone with advanced medical training and equipment take Over_ Medlcal Kit The personal medical kit contains enough bandages and other supplies t0 tend all minor wounds, can also be used to Keep critically wounded character alive long enough tor medic or doctor t0 tend to the wounds: character performing First Aid needs such kit to avoid Lack of Proper Equipment Modifier Clan LSSU The Clans developed more sophisticated version Of the medipack, designated the Life Support Sustaining Unit (LSSU): Initially the L SSUs were only available t0 elementals; but Clan MechWarriors and pilots are now using this device. [PDF_PAGE 209 | score=20] RUNNING THE GAME CONTACTS Contacts are NPCs that character has relationship with thanks to the Contact Trait (see 801. Gamemasters should allow contacts to be mere spigots Of information that play- ers can f and off at willl Contacts should be fully devel- oped characters with their Own desires; goals ad motivations Players should have t0 perform some roleplaying gain infor- mation from then, Or at least succeed appropriate skill rolls (such as Negotiation Protoco Checks). Tne gamemaster should consider whether player charac ler $ contacts with the player character' personality. For example. criminal-turned mercenary character not likely t0 nave grand duke as contact, regardless of how many points the player spends. The players and gamemaster should work out these considerations together As always, the gamemas: ter' $ decision I5 final, but should be fair as well: cash; equipment, promotion or simply the satisfaction 0f well done. However; addition to these tangible and intangible rewards every character will improve through training and prac: tice . [PDF_PAGE 111 | score=21] COMBAT INITIATIVE During turn; characters' phases are performed in cer- tain order; The process of determining the order which phas- es are executed called Initiative. At the beginning of each turn; every character rolls 2010 and adds his RFL Attribute to the result: The total is his Initiative score . Note that this roll is open ended Iike an Action Check; any 10s rolled are rolled again ad added to the Initiative total; Characters take their phases in Initiative order; starting with the character who has the highest Initiative score the event 0i the character with the higher RFL Attribute acts first. Further ties are resolved y an additional tie-breaking die roll; character can choose delay part of his phase atter using part 0i It. For example_ character can Simple Action t0 run into position and then declare that he will use nis second Simple Action make an attack against target of opportunity: the stated conditions do not come up at all in the turn, the character'$ delayed phase moves on into the following turn: does not roll Initiative but continues to delay from the previ- ous turn; phase can be held indefinitely in this way: At the end of any turn; character who is delaying can also choose to stop delaying and roll Initiative normally in the next turn_ When the delaying character takes his phase (or the part of his phase that was delayed), all his Action Checks for that phase have TN modifier of +2 because the character must react using snap judgment: Note that it is impossible for single character take more than one phase in single turn. Combat Sense: Characters with the Combat Sense Trait reduce the delaying modifier to +1_ Combat Paralysis: Characters with the Combat Paralysis Trait increase the delaying modifier to +3. Initiative Modifiers The following skill
[PDF_PAGE 124 | score=12] COMBAT Knockout Check; bleeds every turn] Deadly: Leg severed [WV 12; Knockout Check at +6 TN; Lost Limb (3) Trait; bleeds every turn] HEALING Injured characters will naturally want t0 recover so they can go out ad get into the line of fire again (to the eternal dismay of their physicians). This section describes the process involved treating wounds and what happens to characters their wounds are improperly or incompletely treated Deadly: Arm severed (WV 10; Knockout Check at +6 TN: Lost Limb (31 Trait: bleeds every turn] Chest Minor: Broken ribs [WV 2; +1 to all TNs] Serlous: Minor organ damage [WV 4; Knockout Check; +1 to all TNs] Crltical: Major organ damage ad internal bleeding (WV 8; Knockout Check at +2 TN; +2 t0 all TNs; bleeds every minute] Deadly: Chest cavity crushed heart and lungs destroyed [character instantly killed] FAILED TREATMENT character fumbles any treatment roll (i.e. , rolls 2 on the Action Check dice), the target's condition actually worsens: the case of wounds the wound value of the target wound i5 increased by 1; if he is trying t0 stabilize dying character, the character dies immediately: Foot Minor: Metatarsal bones broken (WV 2; half movement: +1 TN to all RFL-linked Skill Checks] Serlous: Foot crushed [WV 3; half movement] Critical: Foot nearly severed (WV 6; Knockout Check; half movement; +2 TN to all RFL-Iinked Skill Checks; bleeds every minuteE Deadly: Foot destroyed [WV 8; Knockout Check at +5 TN; Lost Limb (2) Trait; bleeds every turn] UNCONSCIOUSNESS Another character can attempt t0 wake up knocked-out character by spending Complex Action. [PDF_PAGE 140 | score=11] EQUIPMENT Flamer Plstol: Flamer pistols are simply cul-down version of the man-pack: Tney suffer from shorter range and much smaller payload but they are favored in some circles because of their smaller size. Heavy Flamer: More akin the flamers used BattleMechs than the ma pack; the heawy flamer covers the same area but Inflicts considerably more damage_ The fuel used even more volatile , making nazardous to its crew: If the crew is hit Dy ranged attack; the gamemaster rolls 2010_ result of 18 greater; the heavy flamer's fuel source explodes and causes the heavy flamer" damage blast effect (damage type X} from the crew as the point of impact: Recoil Compensators Burst-fire weapons are effective, but the recoil generated finng stream of projectiles at high speed massive. You soon moved on t0 advanced studies in vehicle maintenance . while continuing your friendship with your buddies at NAMA Your postgraduate studies took you on tour of the major factories in the Federated Suns that produce civilian and combat vehicles. teaching you many skills you never thought you' possess; In anticipation of one day stepping into your father"' place you took job as vehicle maintenance specialist at Kallon Industries on Nanking: Much to your shock, you hated it; Your lackluster performance ended in horrible coolant accident that permanently damaged most of your skin; You cannot tolerate direct sunlight without complete coverage for more than few hours_ Somewhat disillusioned about civil: ian jobs_ but with Iot of money from the accident settlement, you threw yourself into your first love vehicle maintenance and mastered every aspect of it.
